question.text: To whom must the AGM notice be sent by the RTA, in addition to the members of the company?

answer.text: Only the Statutory Auditors and all Directors
Statutory Auditors, Secretarial Auditors, all Directors, and Debenture Trustee (if any)
Only the Board of Directors and SEBI
Only the Company Secretary and the Managing Director

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The AGM notice must be sent to Members, Statutory Auditors, Secretarial Auditors, all Directors, and the Debenture Trustee (if applicable). This comprehensive distribution ensures all key stakeholders are informed about the meeting and can fulfill their respective roles.
**The Trap:** Candidates often remember that notices go to members and directors but forget the auditors and debenture trustee. The Secretarial Auditor is particularly easy to overlook in the list.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The inclusion of the Debenture Trustee reflects the Companies Act requirement to keep debt security holders' interests represented. This connects to broader NISM II-A topics on corporate governance and stakeholder rights.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Maintain an updated master list of all notice recipients for each issuer company. Include Statutory Auditors, Secretarial Auditors, the full Board, and any Debenture Trustees. Review this list before every AGM to capture any changes in appointments.
